feedback.details: Valid Mermaid C4Context syntax. Cannot verify browser rendering without mmdc CLI. Syntax is correct for all constructs including UpdateLayoutConfig and Boundary definitions.

c1-context.md:line 12 - llm-switch exists as single System but is NOT positioned centrally. Due to UpdateLayoutConfig($c4ShapeInRow='3') on line 38, it is positioned left in the Internal Network boundary with NomadCluster and LocalModelRepository beside it, violating the 'positioned centrally' requirement.

c1-context.md:line 22 - 'AI Application Service' incorrectly modeled as Person_Ext when it is semantically an external system/service, not a person/actor. C4 conventions require System_Ext for external applications/services. This is a fundamental modeling error that misrepresents the architecture.
All four dependencies correctly labeled (lines 13, 14, 18, 19) with directional arrows (lines 28-31). Labels match PRD specification exactly.

c1-context.md:lines 54-69 - All 8 entities (1 system + 3 users + 4 dependencies) explicitly described with checklist. Note: PRD mentions 'six entities' which is mathematically inconsistent (1+3+4=8), but implementation correctly covers all entities.
c1-context.md:lines 71-78 - All PRD requirements explicitly verified. Additional explicit change documentation provided for AI Application Service placement decision.
c1-context.md:lines 42-53 - All seven connection types documented in table with HTTPS/JSON protocols. Includes health check path as eighth relationship.
Specific labels used: 'OpenAI REST API', 'Anthropic REST API', 'Nomad Cluster', 'Local Model Repository'. No generic terms found.

c1-context.md:lines 25-32 - Missing 'model repository availability checks' (criterion item 3). Shows inference request path (line 31) but no explicit health check/availability path to LocalModelRepository. Only shows NomadCluster→llm-switch health probes (line 32). This gap means model repository failures cannot be detected via explicit health monitoring in the diagram.

c1-context.md:lines 11-23 - Two explicit boundaries shown: 'Internal Network' (llm-switch, Nomad, Repository) and 'External Network (Internet)' (APIs and users). Clear demarcation of internal vs external resources.

overall_summary: The architecture is largely complete but fails two critical criteria that would cause production confusion. The 'AI Application Service' is fundamentally mis-modeled as a Person_Ext (external user) when it is clearly an external system/service—this is a C4 modeling violation that will confuse developers implementing the architecture. Additionally, the edge case coverage is incomplete: while fallback paths to OpenAI/Anthropic and Nomad health checks are shown, there is no 'model repository availability check' path, leaving a blind spot for detecting when local models become unavailable. The central system is also not positioned centrally due to the row layout configuration. These issues represent gaps that should be addressed before the architecture can be considered production-ready.
